title: Computer Systems Design Services

alternatives.name: Independent regional project-led computer systems integrator
inclusion_criteria: Primary activity is planning and designing computer systems that integrate hardware, software, and communications technologies, consistent with the official 2022 NAICS 541512 definition.
Revenue is led by systems-design and integration projects; installation, training, and post-implementation support may be included with the integrated engagement.
Firm falls within the deterministic dataset's $1 million to $10 million estimated EBITDA target band, currently mapped to the 20-99 and 100-499 employee classes.
exclusion_criteria: Managed-service-led firms whose primary activity is ongoing remote operation or support.
Public-sector-led integrators whose procurement and contract economics are materially shaped by government customers.
Custom software, on-site facilities management, hosting, and product-resale businesses classified outside 541512 when those activities are primary.
alternatives.rationale: This is the closest operating model to the activity that legally defines 541512. Its count is a bounded allocation of the deterministic 3,524-firm band, not an observed Census cross-tab; the base is made the largest estimate because no narrower model is evidenced as dominant.

alternatives.name: Recurring managed-services-led systems integrator
inclusion_criteria: Provides systems integration but earns most service revenue from recurring managed IT, cloud administration, security, backup, monitoring, or support contracts.
Firm falls within the deterministic target band and reports 541512 because integration remains a principal activity.
exclusion_criteria: Project-led integrators with only ancillary maintenance or support.
Firms more appropriately classified as computer facilities management, other computer-related services, hosting, or data processing.
alternatives.rationale: Managed IT is visibly acquisition-active and overlaps systems integration, but official data do not establish its share inside 541512 or resolve its boundary with 541513, 541519, and 518210.

alternatives.name: Public-sector-led systems integrator
inclusion_criteria: Performs integrated systems design and implementation primarily for federal, state, local, education, or other public-sector customers.
Firm falls within the deterministic target band and its primary establishment activity remains within 541512.
exclusion_criteria: Pure staff augmentation, custom programming, and firms outside the target band.
alternatives.rationale: Government systems integration is a distinct and potentially material model, but public award data do not translate directly into a count of independent 541512 firms in the EBITDA band.

alternatives.name: Vertical or vendor-specialized VAR/integrator
inclusion_criteria: Designs and integrates systems around a vertical workflow or named hardware, software, communications, cloud, or cybersecurity vendor stack.
Integration design is primary even when product resale is a material revenue component, and the firm falls within the deterministic target band.
exclusion_criteria: Wholesalers or retailers for which product resale is the primary activity.
Generalist project-led integrators and managed-service-led firms.
alternatives.rationale: The official definition permits hardware and software within integrated solutions, but the boundary with wholesale and retail trade prevents a precise target-band count.

residual.name: Other or unresolved 541512 target-band operating models
residual.rationale: Residual reconciles the four base allocations to the deterministic 3,524-firm band and covers mixed models that cannot be assigned reliably before firm-level operating-model research.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: 541512 Computer Systems Design Services
2022 Economic Census Industry by Products Statistics
Statistics of U.S. Businesses Data and API
sources.vintage: 2022 SUSB inputs in frozen derived dataset

target_archetype.selection_basis: Apply the deterministic largest-estimated-band-archetype rule to the four mutually exclusive enumerated models. The project-led systems-integrator base count of 1,350 is greater than the managed-services-led estimate of 850, the public-sector-led estimate of 650, and the vertical VAR/integrator estimate of 450; it is therefore selected. This ordering follows official code scope first and does not use downstream economic characteristics. The four base counts plus the 224-firm residual reconcile exactly to the frozen 3,524-firm target band.

value_basis.scope: Target-specific employee and contractor cash-cost share and role-cost mix for independent regional project-led 541512 systems integrators in the $1 million to $10 million estimated EBITDA band.

labor_contractor_cash_cost_share.rationale: Project-led systems integration is a labor-intensive professional service but can include material hardware, software, cloud, and other pass-through revenue. The bounded 40%-70% range and 55% midpoint are a class-neutral feasibility assumption for employee wages plus independent-contractor cash cost as a share of revenue; they are not inferred from the broader 5415 occupational proxy or represented as measured economics.
caveats: No target-archetype source directly measures employee-plus-contractor cash cost as a share of revenue.
Hardware and software resale, subcontractor usage, public-sector exposure, utilization, and recurring-support mix can move the share materially within or beyond the range.
ASSUMPTION/NONE evidence forces UNPROVEN readiness until a defensible target-specific cash-cost source replaces this basis.

roles.title: Systems architecture and solution design
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive cost pool for discovery, requirements, architecture, solution design, and senior technical oversight before and during delivery.

roles.title: Infrastructure and systems implementation engineering
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive cost pool for configuration, deployment, migration, integration testing, network and infrastructure work, and technical remediation.

roles.title: Software configuration and integration specialists
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive cost pool for application configuration, interfaces, scripting, data movement, and limited code needed to integrate systems without redefining the archetype as custom programming.

roles.title: Project management and business analysis
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive cost pool for project planning, coordination, requirements translation, documentation governance, budgeting, and customer delivery management.

roles.title: User support, training, and field service
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive cost pool for installation assistance, user enablement, post-implementation support, troubleshooting, and on-site service tied to integrated engagements.

roles.title: Sales, account management, and solution consulting
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive cost pool for pipeline development, proposals, account coverage, presales solution work not charged to architecture, and commercial coordination.

roles.title: Management and administration
roles.rationale: Assumed mutually exclusive residual employee-cost pool for leadership, finance, people operations, procurement administration, and other corporate support.

value_basis.rationale: This source-free basis is a pre-frozen feasibility assumption for the selected operating model. It supplies a bounded employee-plus-contractor cash-cost share and a complete, mutually exclusive role allocation without claiming that the broader 5415 developer-heavy occupational mix represents project-led 541512 integrators. It must remain ASSUMPTION/NONE and cannot support substantiated readiness.
caveats: All cash-cost and role weights are unsupported assumptions chosen before downstream research; none is a source-backed observation.
The role weights sum to one but do not imply headcount shares, wage shares from OEWS, or task exposure.
The broader 5415 developer-heavy proxy remains rejected for this archetype.
This assumption basis forces UNPROVEN readiness and must be replaced, not relabeled, if defensible target-specific evidence is later found.

source_hints.why: Official 2022 NAICS definition, illustrative examples, and cross-references establish systems-integration design as the primary activity and separate custom programming and product resale.

The 2022 Economic Census industry-by-products dataset can test the revenue importance of systems design, integration, support, resale, and adjacent service lines, although it does not directly identify the $1 million to $10 million EBITDA band.
Frozen deterministic SUSB/QCEW-based dataset provides the 3,524-firm estimated target band, labor share, role proxy, size-class derivation, and source vintages used by the pipeline.

Market report identifies PE-backed roll-up activity, buyer categories, transaction activity, and the operating attributes buyers distinguish within managed IT services; it is useful for testing whether any managed-services alternative is actually comparable to the selected project-led archetype.

Provides disclosed-deal and deal-size segmentation useful for separating small add-ons from larger platforms, but its MSP population must not be assumed to represent project-led 541512 systems integrators.

source_hints.source: Size-matched transaction databases and buyer announcements for U.S. project-led computer systems integrators
source_hints.why: Needed to count demonstrably active consolidators and observe entry and exit pricing for the selected archetype rather than substituting MSP, MSSP, or public-company comparables.

implementation: Does firm-level evidence confirm that systems design and integration projects, rather than recurring managed services, public-sector contracting, resale, custom programming, or hosting, are the selected firms' primary operating model?
What share of target-archetype delivery cost is employee labor, independent-contractor labor, hardware and software procurement, cloud or vendor charges, and other direct cost?

Does the four-digit 5415 occupational proxy materially overstate software development or understate implementation, architecture, field installation, project management, and support roles for the selected archetype?
Which mutually exclusive delivery roles and work activities account for the selected archetype's labor and contractor cash cost?
commercial_retention: What share of target-archetype revenue is fixed-fee project work, time-and-materials, cost-plus or audited-overhead work, product resale, and recurring maintenance or support?

How often can the provider retain implementation productivity through fixed bids or additional capacity, and how often is it passed to clients through fewer billable hours, rebidding, procurement review, or lower renewal pricing?
How do vendor charges, hardware and software pass-through, channel discounts, warranties, service-level commitments, and competitive bidding affect retained operating savings?
sale_availability: What base, low, and high share of the deterministic 3,524-firm band actually satisfies the project-led systems-integrator inclusion criteria?
How many buyers have completed at least one acquisition of a U.S. target-archetype firm within the applicable lookback period, excluding buyers active only in MSP, MSSP, software, hosting, or resale?
Is seller or succession supply demonstrably excess, normal, or unusually scarce for this archetype and size band, without inferring supply from unsupported owner-age anecdotes?
valuation_robustness: What EBITDA multiples are observed for acquisitions of independent U.S. project-led systems integrators in the target band?

What exit multiples are supportable for scaled operators with the same revenue model, and how do size, recurring-support mix, customer concentration, public-sector exposure, and hardware resale affect comparability?
Can entry and exit ranges be supported without importing public-company, MSP, MSSP, software, or unusually large-platform multiples?
operator_survival: At mature AI adoption, does the client continue paying an independent systems integrator to design, implement, secure, and remain accountable for heterogeneous systems?
Which portions of the operator's paid role remain necessary because of architecture, implementation risk, vendor coordination, cybersecurity, compliance, local service, or contractual accountability?
Could hyperscalers, software vendors, distributors, automated platforms, or client IT teams absorb the provider's economic role even if demand for integrated systems continues?
How does mature operator survival differ between project-led integration and the excluded recurring-MSP, resale, staff-augmentation, and custom-development models?

special_notes: Selection is intentionally class-neutral and was made only from official scope, target-band operating-model evidence, and acquisition-market structure.
The research packet must estimate selected-archetype coverage explicitly and must not relabel the deterministic 3,524-firm band as though every firm belongs to the archetype.
Cloud and cybersecurity specialization may occur inside any enumerated model and is not treated as a separate archetype without evidence of a distinct primary operating model.
The broader dataset role proxy is rejected for the selected archetype; the source-free assumption basis forces UNPROVEN readiness until defensible target-specific cash-cost evidence is found.
